In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
%matplotlib inline
import seaborn as sns

# To filter warnings on updated python versions
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Library to split data, impute missing values
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer

# To measure classification performance
from sklearn import metrics
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, classification_report, accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score, roc_auc_score

# To build model for statistical analysis and prediction
import statsmodels.stats.api as sms
from statsmodels.stats.outliers_influence import variance_inflation_factor
import statsmodels.api as sm
from statsmodels.tools.tools import add_constant
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression

from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_percentage_error
In [2]:
df = pd.read_csv("supply_chain_data.csv")

wh_owner_type = df['wh_owner_type'].value_counts()

# Pie chart
plt.figure(figsize=(6, 6))
plt.pie(wh_owner_type, 
        labels=wh_owner_type.index, 
        autopct='%1.1f%%',      
        startangle=90,          
        colors=['lightblue', 'lightcoral', 'lightgreen'])

plt.title('wh_owner_type',pad=20)
plt.axis('equal')
plt.show()

In [19]:
#Dropping warehouse established year column
df.drop('wh_est_year', axis=1, inplace=True)
In [21]:
# Fill numerical column with mean
df['workers_num'].fillna(df['workers_num'].mean(), inplace=True)

# Fill categorical column with mode
df['approved_wh_govt_certificate'].fillna(
    df['approved_wh_govt_certificate'].mode()[0],
    inplace=True
)

In [25]:
# outlier detection using boxplot
numeric_columns = df.select_dtypes(include=np.number).columns.tolist()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 10))

for i, variable in enumerate(numeric_columns):
    plt.subplot(4, 4, i + 1)
    plt.boxplot(df[variable], whis=1.5)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.title(variable)

plt.show()
In [26]:
cols_to_cap = ['transport_issue_l1y', 'Competitor_in_mkt', 'retail_shop_num', 'workers_num', 'flood_impacted', 'flood_proof']

for col in cols_to_cap:
    Q1 = df[col].quantile(0.25)
    Q3 = df[col].quantile(0.75)
    IQR = Q3 - Q1

    lower_limit = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
    upper_limit = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR

    df[col] = df[col].clip(lower_limit, upper_limit)
In [27]:
# outlier detection using boxplot
numeric_columns = df.select_dtypes(include=np.number).columns.tolist()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 10))

for i, variable in enumerate(numeric_columns):
    plt.subplot(4, 4, i + 1)
    plt.boxplot(df[variable], whis=1.5)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.title(variable)

plt.show()
In [28]:
# Data preparation for modeling
# Crating Dummy Data
# drop_first=True will drop one of the three origin columns
df = pd.get_dummies(df, columns=["Location_type", "WH_capacity_size", "zone", "WH_regional_zone", "wh_owner_type", "approved_wh_govt_certificate"], drop_first=True, dtype="uint8")
df.head()

In [30]:
# independent variables
X = df.drop(["product_wg_ton"], axis=1)
# dependent variable
y = df[["product_wg_ton"]]
In [31]:
# let's add the intercept to data
X = sm.add_constant(X)
In [32]:
#Spliting X and y into train and test sets in a 70:30 ratio
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    X, y, test_size=0.30, random_state=1
)
In [33]:
#Fitting Linear Model
olsmod = sm.OLS(y_train, X_train)
olsres = olsmod.fit()
In [34]:
# let's print the regression summary
print(olsres.summary())

                            OLS Regression Results                            
==============================================================================

# let's check the VIF of the predictors
from statsmodels.stats.outliers_influence import variance_inflation_factor

vif_series1 = pd.Series(
    [variance_inflation_factor(X_train.values, i) for i in range(X_train.shape[1])],
    index=X_train.columns,
)
print("VIF values: \n\n{}\n".format(vif_series1))

In [140]:
X_train1 = X_train.drop(["flood_impacted"], axis=1)
olsmod_1 = sm.OLS(y_train, X_train1)
olsres_1 = olsmod_1.fit()
print(
    "R-squared:",
    np.round(olsres_1.rsquared, 3),
    "\nAdjusted R-squared:",
    np.round(olsres_1.rsquared_adj, 3),
)

R-squared: 0.985 
Adjusted R-squared: 0.985

In [190]:
# let us create the dataframe with actual, fitted and residual values
df_pred = pd.DataFrame()

df_pred["Actual Values"] = y_train.values.flatten()  # actual values
df_pred["Fitted Values"] = olsres_30.fittedvalues.values  # predicted values
df_pred["Residuals"] = olsres_30.resid.values  # residuals

df_pred.head()
Out[190]:
Actual Values Fitted Values Residuals
0 23086 23316.395741 -230.395741
1 19108 20325.846383 -1217.846383
2 38125 38728.317177 -603.317177
3 33123 34536.488535 -1413.488535
4 26089 26676.318602 -587.318602
In [192]:
# let us plot the fitted values vs residuals to check Linearity of variables
sns.set_style("whitegrid")
sns.residplot(
    data=df_pred, x="Fitted Values", y="Residuals", color="purple", lowess=True
)
plt.xlabel("Fitted Values")
plt.ylabel("Residuals")
plt.title("Fitted vs Residual plot")
plt.grid(False)
plt.show()

X_train.columns

      dtype='object')
In [196]:
# dropping columns from the test data that are not there in the training data
X_test2 = X_test.drop(
    ['Competitor_in_mkt',
 'retail_shop_num',
 'distributor_num',
 'flood_impacted',
 'flood_proof',
 'electric_supply',
 'dist_from_hub',
 'workers_num',
 'govt_check_l3m',
 'WH_capacity_size_Mid',
 'WH_capacity_size_Small',
 'zone_North',
 'zone_South',
 'zone_West',
 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 2',
 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 3',
 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 4',
 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 5',
 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 6',
 'wh_owner_type_Rented'], axis=1
)
In [197]:
y_pred1 = olsres_30.predict(X_test2)
y_pred1

In [198]:
# let's check the RMSE on the train data
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error
rmse3 = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_train, df_pred["Fitted Values"]))
rmse3
Out[198]:
1405.7512594100854
In [199]:
# let's check the RMSE on the test data
rmse2 = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_test, y_pred1))
rmse2
Out[199]:
1338.7356730648078
In [200]:
#Checking R-Square and Adj.R-Square of test data
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score
r2_test = r2_score(y_test, y_pred1)

n = X_test2.shape[0]
p = X_test2.shape[1]
adj_r2_test = 1 - (1 - r2_test) * (n - 1) / (n - p - 1)

print(f"Test R-squared: {r2_test:.4f}")
print(f"Test Adjusted R-squared: {adj_r2_test:.4f}")

Test R-squared: 0.9865
Test Adjusted R-squared: 0.9865
In [206]:
# let's check the MAE on the train data
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error
mae1 = mean_absolute_error(y_train, df_pred["Fitted Values"])
mae1
Out[206]:
1011.6156126742995
In [207]:
# let's check the MAE on the test data
mae2 = mean_absolute_error(y_test, y_pred1)
mae2
Out[207]:
996.069986428437
In [208]:
# let's check the MAPE on the train data
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_percentage_error

mape1 = mean_absolute_percentage_error(df_pred["Actual Values"], df_pred["Fitted Values"]) * 100
mape1
Out[208]:
7.550651494248699
In [209]:
# let's check the MAPE on the test data
mape2 = mean_absolute_percentage_error(y_test, y_pred1) * 100
mape2
Out[209]:
7.385340299696094
In [210]:
# checking the distribution of variables in training set with dependent variable
sns.pairplot(df[["product_wg_ton",'num_refill_req_l3m', 'transport_issue_l1y',
       'storage_issue_reported_l3m', 'temp_reg_mach', 'wh_breakdown_l3m',
       'Location_type_Urban', 'approved_wh_govt_certificate_A+',
       'approved_wh_govt_certificate_B', 'approved_wh_govt_certificate_B+',
       'approved_wh_govt_certificate_C']])
plt.show()
In [211]:
# using square transformation
X_train["storage_issue_reported_l3m_sq"] = np.square(X_train["storage_issue_reported_l3m"])
X_test2["storage_issue_reported_l3m_sq"] = np.square(X_test2["storage_issue_reported_l3m"])

# let's create a model with the transformed data
olsmod_31 = sm.OLS(y_train, X_train)
olsres_31 = olsmod_31.fit()
print(olsres_31.summary())

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
const                            3202.5207     43.693     73.296      0.000    3116.878    3288.163
num_refill_req_l3m                 25.2289      3.875      6.511      0.000      17.634      32.823
transport_issue_l1y              -371.2989     10.717    -34.647      0.000    -392.305    -350.293
storage_issue_reported_l3m       1024.8519      4.093    250.405      0.000    1016.830    1032.874
temp_reg_mach                     424.8939     24.526     17.324      0.000     376.821     472.967
wh_breakdown_l3m                  -86.5993      6.472    -13.382      0.000     -99.284     -73.914
Location_type_Urban               -13.5448     35.542     -0.381      0.703     -83.210      56.121

approved_wh_govt_certificate_B  -2086.2343     31.211    -66.843      0.000   -2147.411   -2025.058

Omnibus:                     7094.618   Durbin-Watson:                   2.001
Prob(Omnibus):                  0.000   Jarque-Bera (JB):            84144.943
Skew:                           1.616   Prob(JB):                         0.00
Kurtosis:                      13.245   Cond. No.                     3.06e+03

Notes:
[1] Standard Errors assume that the covariance matrix of the errors is correctly specified.
[2] The condition number is large, 3.06e+03. This might indicate that there are
strong multicollinearity or other numerical problems.
In [212]:
# let us create the dataframe with actual, fitted and residual values
df_pred1 = pd.DataFrame()

df_pred1["Actual Values"] = y_train.values.flatten()  # actual values
df_pred1["Fitted Values"] = olsres_31.fittedvalues.values  # predicted values
df_pred1["Residuals"] = olsres_31.resid.values  # residuals

df_pred1.head()
Out[212]:
Actual Values Fitted Values Residuals
0 23086 23022.542290 63.457710
1 19108 19869.505590 -761.505590
2 38125 39074.880795 -949.880795
3 33123 34485.247001 -1362.247001
4 26089 26738.222977 -649.222977
In [213]:
# let us plot the fitted values vs residuals to check Linearity of variables
sns.set_style("whitegrid")
sns.residplot(
    data=df_pred1, x="Fitted Values", y="Residuals", color="purple", lowess=True
)
plt.xlabel("Fitted Values")
plt.ylabel("Residuals")
plt.title("Fitted vs Residual plot")
plt.grid(False)
plt.show()
In [215]:
y_pred2 = olsres_31.predict(X_test2)
y_pred2
Out[215]:

9488      5969.427485
16933    10141.883690

In [217]:
# let's check the RMSE on the train data
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error
rmse4 = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_train, df_pred1["Fitted Values"]))
rmse4
Out[217]:
1277.8834902088429
In [218]:
# let's check the RMSE on the test data
rmse3 = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_test, y_pred2))
rmse3
Out[218]:
1211.6500387306814
In [219]:
#Checking R-Square and Adj.R-Square of test data
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score
r3_test = r2_score(y_test, y_pred2)

n = X_test2.shape[0]
p = X_test2.shape[1]
adj_r3_test = 1 - (1 - r2_test) * (n - 1) / (n - p - 1)

print(f"Test R-squared: {r2_test:.4f}")
print(f"Test Adjusted R-squared: {adj_r2_test:.4f}")

Test R-squared: 0.9865
Test Adjusted R-squared: 0.9865
In [220]:
# let's check the MAE on the train data
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error
mae2 = mean_absolute_error(y_train, df_pred1["Fitted Values"])
mae2
Out[220]:
942.3837674506397
In [221]:
# let's check the MAE on the test data
mae3 = mean_absolute_error(y_test, y_pred2)
mae3
Out[221]:
925.4797329658026
In [222]:
# let's check the MAPE on the train data
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_percentage_error

mape2 = mean_absolute_percentage_error(df_pred1["Actual Values"], df_pred1["Fitted Values"]) * 100
mape2
Out[222]:
6.849352728125524

mape3 = mean_absolute_percentage_error(y_test, y_pred2) * 100

In [224]:
# TEST FOR NORMALITY
sns.histplot(df_pred1["Residuals"], kde=True)
plt.title("Normality of residuals")
plt.grid(False)
plt.show()
In [225]:
#QQ Plot to check normality assumption
import pylab
import scipy.stats as stats

stats.probplot(df_pred1["Residuals"], dist="norm", plot=pylab)
plt.grid(False)
plt.show()
In [226]:
# Shapiro-Wilk test to check normality assumption
stats.shapiro(df_pred1["Residuals"])
Out[226]:
ShapiroResult(statistic=0.916791295194608, pvalue=4.9247217204422494e-70)
In [227]:
# TEST FOR HOMOSCEDASTICITY

import statsmodels.stats.api as sms
from statsmodels.compat import lzip

name = ["F statistic", "p-value"]
test = sms.het_goldfeldquandt(df_pred1["Residuals"], X_train)
lzip(name, test)
Out[227]:
[('F statistic', 0.9595156225067106), ('p-value', 0.9732849714969731)]

In [239]:
# Let us write the equation of linear regression
Equation = "Product_wg_ton ="
print(Equation, end=" ")
for i in range(len(X_train.columns)):
    if i == 0:
        print(olsres_31.params[i], "+", end=" ")
    elif i != len(X_train.columns) - 1:
        print(
            olsres_31.params[i],
            "* (",
            X_train.columns[i],
            ")",
            "+",
            end="  ",
        )
    else:
        print(olsres_31.params[i], "* (", X_train.columns[i], ")")

Product_wg_ton = 3202.52069349862 + 25.228909671525766 * ( num_refill_req_l3m ) +  -371.2989235778026 * ( transport_issue_l1y ) +  1024.8519374250652 * ( storage_issue_reported_l3m ) +  424.89387174768876 * ( temp_reg_mach ) +  -86.59928497589 * ( wh_breakdown_l3m ) +  -13.544751072249625 * ( Location_type_Urban ) +  266.37777747502633 * ( approved_wh_govt_certificate_A+ ) +  -2086.2343113261277 * ( approved_wh_govt_certificate_B ) +  -2093.833264420938 * ( approved_wh_govt_certificate_B+ ) +  -17.476063207667835 * ( approved_wh_govt_certificate_C ) +  6.46202857929141 * ( storage_issue_reported_l3m_sq )
In [43]:
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeRegressor
In [44]:
dTree = DecisionTreeRegressor(random_state=42)
dTree.fit(X_train, y_train)
Out[44]:
DecisionTreeRegressor(random_state=42)
In a Jupyter environment, please rerun this cell to show the HTML representation or trust the notebook.
On GitHub, the HTML representation is unable to render, please try loading this page with nbviewer.org.
DecisionTreeRegressor(random_state=42)
In [47]:
print("Decision Tree Training Performance:")

# Predictions
y_pred_train = dTree.predict(X_train)

# Convert to 1D arrays (avoid shape errors)
y_train_array = np.array(y_train).ravel()
y_pred_array = np.array(y_pred_train).ravel()

# Metrics
rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_train_array, y_pred_array))
mae = mean_absolute_error(y_train_array, y_pred_array)
r2 = r2_score(y_train_array, y_pred_array)

# Adjusted R2
n = X_train.shape[0]   # number of observations
p = X_train.shape[1]   # number of predictors

adj_r2 = 1 - ((1 - r2) * (n - 1) / (n - p - 1))

# MAPE
mape = np.mean(
    np.abs((y_train_array - y_pred_array) /
           np.where(y_train_array == 0, 1, y_train_array))
) * 100

# Print Results
print("RMSE:", rmse)
print("MAE:", mae)
print("R2:", r2)
print("Adjusted R2:", adj_r2)
print("MAPE (%):", mape)

Decision Tree Training Performance:
RMSE: 0.0
MAE: 0.0
R2: 1.0
Adjusted R2: 1.0
MAPE (%): 0.0
In [49]:
print("Decision Tree Test Performance:")

# Predictions
y_pred_test = dTree.predict(X_test)

# Convert to 1D arrays
y_test_array = np.array(y_test).ravel()
y_pred_array = np.array(y_pred_test).ravel()

# Metrics
rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_test_array, y_pred_array))
mae = mean_absolute_error(y_test_array, y_pred_array)
r2 = r2_score(y_test_array, y_pred_array)

# Adjusted R2
n = X_test.shape[0]
p = X_test.shape[1]

adj_r2 = 1 - ((1 - r2) * (n - 1) / (n - p - 1))

#MAPE
mape = np.mean(np.abs((y_test_array - y_pred_array) / np.where(y_test_array == 0, 1, y_test_array))) * 100

print("RMSE:", rmse)
print("MAE:", mae)
print("R2:", r2)
print("Adjusted R2:", adj_r2)
print("MAPE (%):", mape)

Decision Tree Test Performance:
RMSE: 1288.979946883064
MAE: 873.6882666666667
R2: 0.9874799347567806
Adjusted R2: 0.9874279634093597
MAPE (%): 5.485179912237107
In [51]:
feature_names = list(X.columns)
print(feature_names)

importances = dTree.feature_importances_
indices = np.argsort(importances)

plt.figure(figsize=(12,12))
plt.title('Feature Importances')
plt.barh(range(len(indices)), importances[indices], color='violet', align='center')
plt.yticks(range(len(indices)), [feature_names[i] for i in indices])
plt.xlabel('Relative Importance')
plt.show()

['const', 'num_refill_req_l3m', 'transport_issue_l1y', 'Competitor_in_mkt', 'retail_shop_num', 'distributor_num', 'flood_impacted', 'flood_proof', 'electric_supply', 'dist_from_hub', 'workers_num', 'storage_issue_reported_l3m', 'temp_reg_mach', 'wh_breakdown_l3m', 'govt_check_l3m', 'Location_type_Urban', 'WH_capacity_size_Mid', 'WH_capacity_size_Small', 'zone_North', 'zone_South', 'zone_West', 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 2', 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 3', 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 4', 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 5', 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 6', 'wh_owner_type_Rented', 'approved_wh_govt_certificate_A+', 'approved_wh_govt_certificate_B', 'approved_wh_govt_certificate_B+', 'approved_wh_govt_certificate_C']
In [53]:
from sklearn.ensemble import BaggingRegressor

#base_estimator for bagging Regressor is a decision tree by default
bagging_estimator=BaggingRegressor(random_state=1)
bagging_estimator.fit(X_train,y_train)
Out[53]:
BaggingRegressor(random_state=1)

In [54]:
print("Bagging Training Performance:")

# Predictions
y_pred_train = bagging_estimator.predict(X_train)

# Convert to 1D arrays (avoid shape errors)
y_train_array = np.array(y_train).ravel()
y_pred_array = np.array(y_pred_train).ravel()

# Metrics
rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_train_array, y_pred_array))
mae = mean_absolute_error(y_train_array, y_pred_array)
r2 = r2_score(y_train_array, y_pred_array)

# Adjusted R2
n = X_train.shape[0]   # number of observations
p = X_train.shape[1]   # number of predictors

adj_r2 = 1 - ((1 - r2) * (n - 1) / (n - p - 1))

# MAPE
mape = np.mean(
    np.abs((y_train_array - y_pred_array) /
           np.where(y_train_array == 0, 1, y_train_array))
) * 100

# Print Results
print("RMSE:", rmse)
print("MAE:", mae)
print("R2:", r2)
print("Adjusted R2:", adj_r2)
print("MAPE (%):", mape)

R2: 0.9986707171834899

    np.abs((y_train_array - y_pred_array) /
           np.where(y_train_array == 0, 1, y_train_array))
) * 100

y_pred_test = Gbr.predict(X_test)

y_pred_array = np.array(y_pred_test).ravel()

# Metrics
rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_test_array, y_pred_array))
mae = mean_absolute_error(y_test_array, y_pred_array)
r2 = r2_score(y_test_array, y_pred_array)

# Adjusted R2
n = X_test.shape[0]
p = X_test.shape[1]

adj_r2 = 1 - ((1 - r2) * (n - 1) / (n - p - 1))

#MAPE
mape = np.mean(np.abs((y_test_array - y_pred_array) / np.where(y_test_array == 0, 1, y_test_array))) * 100

print("RMSE:", rmse)
print("MAE:", mae)
print("R2:", r2)
print("Adjusted R2:", adj_r2)
print("MAPE (%):", mape)

GradientBoost Test Performance:
RMSE: 909.7981927734324
MAE: 693.8379133008814
R2: 0.9937625883114238
Adjusted R2: 0.9937366965382121
MAPE (%): 4.578556386121584
In [51]:
from sklearn.model_selection import GridSearchCV
from sklearn import metrics
In [69]:
# Choose the type of Regressor.
bagging_tuned = BaggingRegressor(
    random_state=1,
    n_jobs=-1   # parallel inside bagging
)

# Reduced but effective parameter grid
parameters = {
    'n_estimators': [100, 200],
    'max_samples': [0.8, 1.0], 
    'max_features': [0.7, 0.9]
}

# GridSearch with faster settings
grid_obj = GridSearchCV(
    bagging_tuned,
    parameters,
    scoring='neg_root_mean_squared_error',
    cv=3, 
    n_jobs=-1 
)

grid_obj.fit(X_train, y_train)

# Best model
bagging_tuned = grid_obj.best_estimator_

# Fit final model
bagging_tuned.fit(X_train, y_train)

print("Best Parameters:", grid_obj.best_params_)

Best Parameters: {'max_features': 0.9, 'max_samples': 1.0, 'n_estimators': 100}
In [70]:
# Set the reg to the best combination of parameters
bagging_tuned1 = BaggingRegressor(
    max_samples=1.0,
    max_features=0.9,
    n_estimators =100
)

# Fit the best algorithm to the data
bagging_tuned1.fit(X_train, y_train)
Out[70]:

BaggingRegressor(max_features=0.9, n_estimators=100)
In [71]:
print("Bagging Tuned Training Performance:")

# Predictions
y_pred_train = bagging_tuned1.predict(X_train)

# Convert to 1D arrays (avoid shape errors)
y_train_array = np.array(y_train).ravel()
y_pred_array = np.array(y_pred_train).ravel()

# Metrics
rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_train_array, y_pred_array))
mae = mean_absolute_error(y_train_array, y_pred_array)
r2 = r2_score(y_train_array, y_pred_array)

# Adjusted R2
n = X_train.shape[0]   # number of observations
p = X_train.shape[1]   # number of predictors

adj_r2 = 1 - ((1 - r2) * (n - 1) / (n - p - 1))

# MAPE
mape = np.mean(
    np.abs((y_train_array - y_pred_array) /
           np.where(y_train_array == 0, 1, y_train_array))
) * 100

# Print Results
print("RMSE:", rmse)
print("MAE:", mae)
print("R2:", r2)
print("Adjusted R2:", adj_r2)
print("MAPE (%):", mape)

Bagging Tuned Training Performance:
RMSE: 603.7093854783395
MAE: 465.3524417142857
R2: 0.9973122516259717
Adjusted R2: 0.9973074817496496
MAPE (%): 3.3027843932212724

# Convert to 1D arrays

y_pred_array = np.array(y_pred_test).ravel()

# Metrics

mae = mean_absolute_error(y_test_array, y_pred_array)
r2 = r2_score(y_test_array, y_pred_array)

# Adjusted R2
n = X_test.shape[0]
p = X_test.shape[1]

adj_r2 = 1 - ((1 - r2) * (n - 1) / (n - p - 1))

#MAPE
mape = np.mean(np.abs((y_test_array - y_pred_array) / np.where(y_test_array == 0, 1, y_test_array))) * 100

print("RMSE:", rmse)
print("MAE:", mae)
print("R2:", r2)
print("Adjusted R2:", adj_r2)

In [73]:
# Choose the type of Regressor.
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestRegressor
from sklearn.model_selection import RandomizedSearchCV

rf_estimator_tuned= RandomForestRegressor(
    random_state=1,
    bootstrap=True,
    n_jobs=-1
)

param_dist = {
    "n_estimators": [200, 300, 400],
    "max_depth": [15, 20, 25, None],
    "min_samples_split": [2, 5],
    "min_samples_leaf": [1, 2],
    "max_features": [0.6, 0.8],
    "max_samples": [0.7, 0.8]
}

random_search = RandomizedSearchCV(
    rf_estimator_tuned,
    param_distributions=param_dist,
    n_iter=25,                     # only 25 models instead of 972
    scoring='neg_root_mean_squared_error',
    cv=3,
    n_jobs=-1,
    random_state=1,
    verbose=1
)

random_search.fit(X_train, y_train)

rf_estimator_tuned = random_search.best_estimator_

print("Best Parameters:", random_search.best_params_)

Fitting 3 folds for each of 25 candidates, totalling 75 fits
Best Parameters: {'n_estimators': 400, 'min_samples_split': 5, 'min_samples_leaf': 2, 'max_samples': 0.7, 'max_features': 0.8, 'max_depth': 15}
In [74]:
# Set the reg to the best combination of parameters
rf_estimator_tuned1 = RandomForestRegressor(
 random_state=1,
 max_depth= 15,
 max_features= 0.8,
 max_samples= 0.7,
 min_impurity_decrease= 0.002,
 min_samples_split=2,   
 min_samples_leaf= 2,
 n_estimators= 400
)

# Fit the best algorithm to the data
rf_estimator_tuned1.fit(X_train, y_train)

print("RandomForest Tuned Training Performance:")

# Predictions
y_pred_train = rf_estimator_tuned1.predict(X_train)

# Convert to 1D arrays (avoid shape errors)
y_train_array = np.array(y_train).ravel()
y_pred_array = np.array(y_pred_train).ravel()

# Metrics
rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_train_array, y_pred_array))
mae = mean_absolute_error(y_train_array, y_pred_array)
r2 = r2_score(y_train_array, y_pred_array)

# Adjusted R2
n = X_train.shape[0]   # number of observations
p = X_train.shape[1]   # number of predictors

adj_r2 = 1 - ((1 - r2) * (n - 1) / (n - p - 1))

# MAPE
mape = np.mean(
    np.abs((y_train_array - y_pred_array) /
           np.where(y_train_array == 0, 1, y_train_array))
) * 100

# Print Results
print("RMSE:", rmse)
print("MAE:", mae)
print("R2:", r2)
print("Adjusted R2:", adj_r2)
print("MAPE (%):", mape)

In [53]:
gbr_tuned = GradientBoostingRegressor(random_state=1)

# Parameter grid
parameters = {
    "n_estimators": [200, 300],
    "learning_rate": [0.05, 0.1],
    "max_depth": [3, 5],
    "subsample": [0.8, 1],
    "max_features": [0.7, 0.9]
}

# Grid Search
grid_obj = GridSearchCV(
    gbr_tuned,
    parameters,
    scoring='neg_root_mean_squared_error',
    cv=3,
    n_jobs=-1,
    verbose=1
)

grid_obj.fit(X_train, y_train)

# Best model
gbr_tuned = grid_obj.best_estimator_

# Fit model
gbr_tuned.fit(X_train, y_train)

# Best parameters
print("Best Parameters:", grid_obj.best_params_)

Fitting 3 folds for each of 32 candidates, totalling 96 fits
Best Parameters: {'learning_rate': 0.05, 'max_depth': 5, 'max_features': 0.9, 'n_estimators': 200, 'subsample': 0.8}
In [55]:
# Set the reg to the best combination of parameters
gbr_tuned1 = GradientBoostingRegressor(
 random_state=1,
 learning_rate= 0.05,
 max_depth= 5,
 max_features= 0.9,
 n_estimators= 200,
 subsample=0.8   
)

# Fit the best algorithm to the data
gbr_tuned1.fit(X_train, y_train)

print("GradientBoosting Tuned Training Performance:")

# Predictions
y_pred_train = gbr_tuned1.predict(X_train)

# Convert to 1D arrays (avoid shape errors)
y_train_array = np.array(y_train).ravel()
y_pred_array = np.array(y_pred_train).ravel()

# Metrics
rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_train_array, y_pred_array))
mae = mean_absolute_error(y_train_array, y_pred_array)
r2 = r2_score(y_train_array, y_pred_array)

# Adjusted R2
n = X_train.shape[0]   # number of observations
p = X_train.shape[1]   # number of predictors

adj_r2 = 1 - ((1 - r2) * (n - 1) / (n - p - 1))

# MAPE
mape = np.mean(
    np.abs((y_train_array - y_pred_array) /
           np.where(y_train_array == 0, 1, y_train_array))
) * 100

# Print Results
print("RMSE:", rmse)
print("MAE:", mae)
print("R2:", r2)
print("Adjusted R2:", adj_r2)
print("MAPE (%):", mape)

p = X_test.shape[1]

In [61]:
feature_names = list(X.columns)
print(feature_names)

importances = gbr_tuned1.feature_importances_
indices = np.argsort(importances)

plt.figure(figsize=(12,12))
plt.title('Feature Importances')
plt.barh(range(len(indices)), importances[indices], color='violet', align='center')
plt.yticks(range(len(indices)), [feature_names[i] for i in indices])
plt.xlabel('Relative Importance')
plt.show()

['const', 'num_refill_req_l3m', 'transport_issue_l1y', 'Competitor_in_mkt', 'retail_shop_num', 'distributor_num', 'flood_impacted', 'flood_proof', 'electric_supply', 'dist_from_hub', 'workers_num', 'storage_issue_reported_l3m', 'temp_reg_mach', 'wh_breakdown_l3m', 'govt_check_l3m', 'Location_type_Urban', 'WH_capacity_size_Mid', 'WH_capacity_size_Small', 'zone_North', 'zone_South', 'zone_West', 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 2', 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 3', 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 4', 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 5', 'WH_regional_zone_Zone 6', 'wh_owner_type_Rented', 'approved_wh_govt_certificate_A+', 'approved_wh_govt_certificate_B', 'approved_wh_govt_certificate_B+', 'approved_wh_govt_certificate_C']
